title: 8 Herbal Tinctures For Difficulty Chewing
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures can be a helpful solution for people who struggle with chewing. These tinctures are made from concentrated plant extracts that can help reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the mouth and jaw. When we have difficulty chewing, it can be frustrating and painful, making everyday tasks like eating and speaking challenging. Herbal teas, in particular, can provide relief due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe sore muscles and joints.  For example, Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root, has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce swelling and pain in the mouth and jaw. Zingiber officinale, or ginger, has similar properties that can help relax the muscles and reduce inflammation. Curcuma longa, or turmeric, contains a powerful anti-inflammatory compound called curcumin, which can help reduce pain and inflammation.  Using these herbal tinctures can bring many benefits to your life. For one, you may be able to eat and drink more comfortably, which can improve your nutrition and overall health. You may also be able to speak more clearly and confidently, which can help you communicate more effectively with others. Additionally, herbal tinctures can be a natural and non-invasive way to manage pain and inflammation, which can reduce your reliance on medication. By incorporating herbal teas into your daily routine, you may be able to find relief from difficulty chewing and improve your overall quality of life.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures contains the bioactive constituents glycyrrhizin and flavonoids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the mouth and throat. Glycyrrhizin, in particular, has been shown to have expectorant properties, which can help thin mucus and make it easier to swallow. The flavonoids in Glycyrrhiza glabra tincture also have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the tissues in the mouth and throat from damage and promote healing. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Glycyrrhiza glabra tincture can help make chewing and swallowing easier for people with difficulty chewing.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal tinctures for difficulty chewing
remedy_procedure: Gather 250ml of vodka and 50g of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root.
Combine vodka and dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar.
Label and store the tincture in a cool, dark place. Use 15-30 drops, 3-4 times a day, as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tinctures contains active compounds like gingerols and shogaols. These constituents have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and ease pain in the jaw and face. The warming and soothing effect of Zingiber officinale tinctures can also help relax tense muscles, making it easier to chew and swallow. Additionally, ginger's antioxidant properties can help protect the mouth and throat from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healing and reducing discomfort. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, Zingiber officinale tinctures can help alleviate difficulty chewing caused by various conditions.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal tinctures for difficulty chewing
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Zingiber officinale roots and 2 cups of 80% ethanol (vodka or rum work well)
Chop the roots into small pieces and put them in a clean glass jar
Pour the ethanol over the roots, making sure they are completely covered
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa tinctures contains curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin, which are powerful bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce swelling and pain in the mouth and jaw. Curcumin, in particular, has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, which can contribute to difficulties in chewing. The anti-inflammatory properties of curcuma longa tinctures also help to soothe and relax the muscles in the jaw and face, making it easier to chew and swallow. By reducing inflammation and pain, curcuma longa tinctures can help to alleviate the discomfort and difficulty associated with chewing.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal tinctures for difficulty chewing
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Curcuma longa root and 2 cups of 80% ethanol or vodka in a clean glass jar.
Combine the dried root and ethanol in the jar, making sure the root is fully covered.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in a cool, dark place and take 10-20 drops, 2-3 times a day, as needed to help with difficulty chewing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tinctures contains flavoglycosides and bilobalide, which are active constituents that can help with difficulty chewing. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling in the mouth and throat, making it easier to eat and chew. The flavoglycosides also improve blood flow to the gums and teeth, promoting healthy oral tissues and reducing pain. Bilobalide has antioxidant properties that protect the gums from damage and promote healing. By reducing inflammation and promoting healthy oral tissues, Ginkgo biloba tinctures can help alleviate difficulty chewing and improve oral health.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal tinctures for difficulty chewing
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, a clean glass jar with a lid, a coffee filter, and 2 cups of vodka.
Combine Ginkgo biloba leaves and vodka in the glass jar, making sure the leaves are completely covered.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Store the Ginkgo biloba tincture in a cool, dark place and take 20-30 drops, 3 times a day, as needed to help with difficulty chewing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis tinctures contains the active constituents quinine and cinchonine, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help reduce swelling and pain in the muscles and joints, making it easier to chew and swallow. Quinine also acts as a vasodilator, improving blood flow to the affected areas, which can help alleviate pain and stiffness. The antioxidant properties of cinchonine protect the mouth and throat from damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and discomfort. By reducing inflammation and pain, Cinchona officinalis tinctures can help make eating and chewing more comfortable.
remedies.image_alt: Cinchona officinalis herbal tinctures for difficulty chewing
remedy_procedure: Gather materials: 1 cup of Cinchona officinalis bark, 2 cups of 80-proof vodka or glycerin, a clean glass jar, and a cheesecloth.
Dry the Cinchona officinalis bark in a low-temperature oven (150°F) for 1 hour. Then, chop it into small pieces.
Combine the chopped Cinchona officinalis bark and 2 cups of vodka or glycerin in the glass jar. Stir well to coat the bark.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it daily. This allows the tincture to mature.

Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another container, discarding the solids. Use the tincture as needed for difficulty chewing, following the recommended dosage (typically 20-30 drops in water or tea).

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tinctures contains several bioactive constituents that help with difficulty chewing. The main bioactive compounds include alkylamides, glycosides, and phenolic acids. Alkylamides stimulate the secretion of saliva, which helps to break down food and make it easier to chew. Glycosides have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and pain in the mouth and jaw, making it easier to chew. Phenolic acids also have anti-inflammatory properties and can help to soothe and calm irritated tissues in the mouth.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal tinctures for difficulty chewing
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Echinacea purpurea root and flowers, 2 cups of vodka, and a clean glass jar.
Combine the dried Echinacea root and flowers in the glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of vodka over the Echinacea mixture, making sure everything is covered.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another glass jar, and discard the solids. Use the tincture as needed to help with difficulty chewing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng tinctures contains a high concentration of bioactive compounds like ginsenosides, ginsenolic acids, and saponins. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the mouth and jaw, making it easier to chew. The ginsenosides in Panax ginseng tinctures also have antioxidant properties that protect the gums and mucous membranes from damage, promoting healthy tissue and reducing discomfort. The saponins in Panax ginseng tinctures have a soothing effect on the mouth and throat, reducing inflammation and pain associated with difficulty chewing. By reducing inflammation and promoting healthy tissue, Panax ginseng tinctures can help alleviate difficulty chewing caused by conditions like temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders or oral surgery.
remedies.image_alt: Panax ginseng herbal tinctures for difficulty chewing
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of 80-proof vodka and 1/2 cup of dried Panax ginseng root in a clean glass jar.
Fill a glass cup with water and add 1 tablespoon of dried Panax ginseng root. Steep for 10-15 minutes. Strain the water and add the steeped ginseng to the jar with vodka.
Close the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks. Shake the jar every day to help the mixture.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Transfer the tincture to small dropper bottles. Use 20-30 drops of the tincture as needed to help with difficulty chewing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis tinctures contains carnosic acid and rosmarinic acid as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the mouth and throat, making it easier to chew. The antispasmodic properties of rosmarinic acid can also help to relax the muscles in the jaw and face, reducing tension and difficulty when eating. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of carnosic acid can help to prevent infections in the mouth and throat, which can contribute to difficulty chewing. By reducing inflammation, relaxing muscles, and preventing infections, Rosmarinus officinalis tinctures can help to alleviate the symptoms associated with difficulty chewing.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal tinctures for difficulty chewing
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Rosmarinus officinalis (rosemary) leaves and 2 cups of 80-proof vodka or glycerin.
Combine the rosemary leaves and vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar. Stir well.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks. Shake the jar every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Label and date the jar. Store it in a cool, dark place. Use 10-20 drops of the tincture as needed to help with difficulty chewing.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent difficulty chewing, also known as dysphagia. Certain teas, like ginger and peppermint, can aid digestion and reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat. This can ease the swallowing process and make it easier to chew food. Regularly drinking these teas may help maintain a healthy oral and digestive system.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can be a helpful addition to your daily routine if you have difficulty chewing. Many herbal teas, like peppermint and ginger, are gentle on the digestive system and can help soothe mouth and throat irritation. However, it's essential to choose teas from reputable sources to ensure they're free from contaminants and made from high-quality herbs.
faq_timeline: It can take a few weeks for herbal teas to start showing results in making it easier to chew. Some people notice a difference in a week or two, while others might take a bit longer. The time it takes depends on the individual and the specific herbs being used.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for difficulty chewing can be most helpful when you're getting ready for bed. Sipping on a soothing tea like peppermint or chamomile can calm your mouth and jaw, making it easier to relax and chew more comfortably. The warmth of the tea can also help ease any pain or tension.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for difficulty chewing. Some herbal teas, like ginkgo biloba, may enhance the effects of medications that thin the blood, increasing the risk of bleeding. Others, like peppermint, can slow down the movement of food through the digestive system, making chewing and swallowing harder.
faq_pregnant: During pregnancy, it's a good idea to check the ingredients of herbal teas before drinking them. Some herbs, like pennyroyal and blue cohosh, can be harmful to the baby. Look for teas labeled as "safe for pregnancy" or choose peppermint, chamomile, and ginger teas that are generally considered okay.
faq_breastfeeding: If you're breastfeeding and having trouble chewing, herbal teas might sound like a soothing solution. However, some herbs can pass into breast milk and affect your baby. Peppermint, chamomile, and ginger teas are often considered safe, but it's essential to check the ingredients and avoid any that could harm your baby.
